_COMMENT_TOP: ThreatWatch AI Brief Template. Copy and rename this file for each new brief. _COMMENT keys are ignored by the renderer.

cfg.classification: Open-source intelligence only  |  Not for redistribution
cfg.audience: Security/executive leadership, travel, supply chain, business continuity

sourceRegistry._COMMENT: Add event-specific source IDs here. Built-in IDs (CENTCOM, IDF, REUTERS, etc.) never need registration.

EXAMPLE_SRC.label: Example Official Statement via Press Conference

execSummary._COMMENT: Position 1: Most important CONFIRMED development. 2-3 sentences. State what happened and its operational implication.
execSummary.text: REPLACE: Most important confirmed development and its immediate operational implication in 2-3 sentences.
REPLACE: Second confirmed development and its immediate implication.

execSummary._COMMENT: Position 3: Most likely near-term trajectory (assessment, not fact).
execSummary.text: REPLACE: Most likely near-term trajectory based on available evidence. State the basis.
REPLACE: The primary operational risk for the target audience (travel, supply chain, cyber, etc.).

execSummary._COMMENT: Position 5: Single most important unresolved variable. Name the fork in the road. No conf/sources needed.
execSummary.text: REPLACE: The single most important unresolved question. What are the two possible outcomes and what observable event resolves it?

sections._COMMENT: Standard order: Leadership/Political → Military → Infrastructure/Travel → International Reactions. Add/remove as needed.

items._COMMENT: Primary content block. One development per claim.
items.text: REPLACE: The specific development. Who did what, when, where, according to whom.

items._COMMENT: Use evidenceBox for 2-4 highest-stakes claims that will be challenged. Teal-bordered provenance box.
items.claim: Short restatement of the specific claim being documented.

rows.label: Date/time
rows.value: When the statement or event occurred

rows.label: Basis/quote
rows.value: Exact quote or stated factual basis

rows.label: Confidence
rows.value: Why HIGH/MED/LOW and what would change this rating

items._COMMENT: factJudge: two-column table. Teal = confirmed facts, Purple = analyst judgments. Place at end of major subsection.
facts: Confirmed fact #1 with inline source citation. [SOURCE]
judgments: Analyst judgment tied to fact #1. State confidence and what would change it. [CONF:MED]

items._COMMENT: Analyst callout box. Styles: info, analyst, warning, alert, t4, scope, purple
items.label: ANALYST ASSESSMENT: TOPIC [CONF: MED]
text: Second paragraph. State what would change this assessment.

scorecard._COMMENT: 8-12 rows max. Status vocabulary: CONFIRMED / NOT YET / BUILDING / CRITICAL / ELEVATED / UNKNOWN / ONGOING / HALTED
scorecard.watch: What observable event would change this status?
Next confirmed strike or ceasefire signal
Back-channel contact confirmed or denied within 24 hrs
Port closure announcement or major carrier halt

actions._COMMENT: 6-10 items per group max. Every action must be specific enough to execute and time-bounded. Omit a group entirely if no actions apply.
travel: REPLACE: Specific travel action tied to a risk in the sections above. Implicit timeframe: next 24-48h.
Example: Suspend all travel to [region] immediately. Direct any staff in [region] to shelter in place and contact [contact].
supplyChain: Example: If procurement triggers set at $X/bbl, prepare to execute hedging. Contact logistics vendors before [market open].
cyber: REPLACE: Specific cyber action tied to threat actor or vector identified in sections above.
Example: Elevate monitoring for phishing and intrusion attempts. Brief finance/procurement staff on [specific fraud type] now.
comms: REPLACE: Specific communications or leadership action.
Example: Brief board/risk committee within next business day. Prepare holding statement if organization has exposure to [issue].

parallel._COMMENT: Optional parallel developments section. Set notApplicable:true to render a placeholder, or fill title/rationale/items.

sourcesAppendix._COMMENT: List all sources used, by tier. Omit a tier block if none used.
t1: REPLACE: Official statement or primary source — date range
t2: REPLACE: Reuters, AP, CNN, [other T2 outlets] — live updates and breaking news coverage
t3: REPLACE: CSIS, CFR, [think-tank] — specific report or dispatch
t4: REPLACE: [Channel/account] — specific claim (CONF:LOW); state information operations incentive
